docs.title: CVM colorBot Installation Guide
docs.text:
Shared guide index: README.md

This guide will help you complete the full installation and setup process for CVM colorBot.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before starting the CVM colorBot installation, ensure your system meets the following requirements:

  • Operating System: Windows 10 or higher
  • Python: Python 3.11 or higher (Python 3.11.9 recommended)
  • Internet Connection: Required for downloading dependency packages (during first installation)

If you haven't installed Python yet, please refer to the Python Installation Guide to complete Python installation first.

docs.title: Step 1: Download the Project
docs.text:
1. Download or clone the CVM colorBot project from GitHub.
  2. Extract the project to your chosen directory (e.g., D:\CVM-colorBot-main).
  3. Verify the project directory contains the following files:
  4. main.py (main program entry point)
  5. setup.bat (automated installation script)
  6. run.bat (application launcher script)
  7. requirements.txt (dependency package list)

docs.title: Step 2: Run the Automated Installation Script
docs.text:
1. Locate the setup.bat file in the project root directory.

  2. Double-click to run setup.bat, or execute it in Command Prompt: 

    setup.bat

  3. The installation script will automatically perform the following operations:

  4. Check if Python is installed and in PATH
  5. Create a virtual environment (venv folder)
  6. Upgrade pip to the latest version

  7. Install all necessary dependency packages

  8. Wait for the installation to complete. The installation process may take several minutes, depending on your network speed.

docs.title: Step 3: Verify Installation
docs.text:
After installation completes, verify the following items:

  1. Check Virtual Environment:
  2. Confirm the venv folder has been created in the project directory

  3. Confirm the venv\Scripts\ directory exists

  4. Check Dependency Packages (optional): In Command Prompt, execute: 

    venv\Scripts\activate
pip list
    You should see the following main packages:

  5. customtkinter
  6. numpy
  7. opencv-python
  8. pyserial
  9. mss
  10. And other dependency packages

docs.title: Step 4: Launch the Application
docs.text: After installation, you can launch CVM colorBot using the following methods:

docs.title: Method 1: Using the Launcher Script (Recommended)
docs.text:
1. Double-click the run.bat file in the project root directory.
  2. The application will automatically launch using Python from the virtual environment.

docs.title: Method 2: Manual Launch
docs.text:
1. Open Command Prompt (CMD) or PowerShell.
  2. Navigate to the project directory: 
    cd D:\CVM-colorBot-main
  3. Launch the application: 
    venv\Scripts\python.exe main.py

docs.title: Step 5: Initial Configuration
docs.text:
When launching CVM colorBot for the first time, it's recommended to perform the following basic configuration:

  1. Select Capture Mode:
  2. In the General tab, select an appropriate capture mode
  3. Available modes: UDP, NDI, Capture Card, MSS

  4. For detailed setup, refer to the corresponding setup guides:

    • OBS UDP Setup
    • MAKCU Setup

  5. Configure Mouse API:

  6. In the General tab, select Input API
  7. If using MAKCU device, select Serial (unless using 3.8 left (firewall) firmware or makxdV2 hardware, then select MakV2Binary)
  8. Configure related connection parameters (e.g., COM port, baud rate, etc.)

  9. For detailed setup, refer to MAKCU Setup

  10. Adjust Basic Settings:

  11. Note: FOV size is adjusted in OBS (refer to OBS UDP Setup and FOV Size Reference), not in CVM
  12. Configure aimbot mode and parameters in CVM
  13. Enable or disable features as needed

docs.title: Issue 1: Python Not Found
docs.text:
Error Message: [ERROR] Python is not installed or not in PATH

Solution: 1. Verify Python is correctly installed (run python --version to check) 2. Verify Python is added to system PATH 3. Refer to the Python Installation Guide to reinstall Python

docs.title: Issue 2: Virtual Environment Creation Failed
docs.text:
Error Message: [ERROR] Failed to create virtual environment

Solution: 1. Verify you have sufficient disk space 2. Verify the project directory has write permissions 3. Try running setup.bat as Administrator 4. Check if Python installation is complete

docs.title: Issue 3: Dependency Package Installation Failed
docs.text:
Error Message: [ERROR] Failed to install dependencies

Solution: 1. Check if network connection is working 2. Verify pip is updated to the latest version: 

python -m pip install --upgrade pip
3. Try manually installing dependency packages: 
venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
4. If a specific package installation fails, try installing it individually: 
pip install <package-name>

docs.title: Issue 4: Application Won't Start
docs.text:
Error Message: [ERROR] Program exited with error

Solution: 1. Verify the virtual environment was created correctly 2. Verify all dependency packages are installed correctly 3. Check if config.json file exists and has correct format 4. Review detailed error messages in console output 5. Verify system meets all prerequisites

docs.title: Issue 5: Module Import Error
docs.text:
Error Message: ModuleNotFoundError or ImportError

Solution: 1. Verify you're using Python from the virtual environment: 

venv\Scripts\python.exe main.py
2. Reinstall missing packages: 
venv\Scripts\activate
pip install <missing-package-name>
3. If the problem persists, try running setup.bat again

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
After installation, it's recommended to:

  1. Read Related Setup Guides:
  2. OBS UDP Setup - Configure UDP capture mode
  3. MAKCU Setup - Configure MAKCU hardware device
  4. Kmbox Net Connection Guide - Configure Kmbox Net device

  5. FOV Size Reference - Understand FOV size settings

  6. Familiarize Yourself with the Application Interface:

  7. Explore features in each tab
  8. Understand the purpose of each configuration option

  9. Test basic functions to ensure they work properly

  10. Optimize Settings:

  11. Adjust parameters based on your hardware and needs
  12. Save configuration files for future use
  13. Test different modes to find the most suitable settings

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• Windows Only: CVM colorBot is designed for Windows and does not support other operating systems
  • Regular Updates: It's recommended to regularly check and update the project and dependency packages
  • Backup Configuration: Regularly backup your config.json and configuration files
  • System Permissions: Some features may require Administrator privileges (e.g., serial port access)

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
If you encounter issues during installation:

  1. Check the "Troubleshooting" section in this document
  2. Review the project's GitHub Issues
  3. Verify your system and Python version meet requirements
  4. Provide detailed error messages for problem diagnosis

docs.title: FOV Size Reference
docs.text:
Shared guide index: README.md

Use these tables to map base resolution to cropped resolution.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
Base Resolution corresponds to your desktop or game resolution. Find the table that matches your desktop resolution, then select your desired FOV value.

Example: - If your desktop resolution is 2560x1440, refer to the "## 2560x1440" table - Then select your desired FOV value, for example 128 - The corresponding Cropped Resolution is 1216x656, which are the crop values you need to fill in the OBS Crop/Pad filter

docs.title: Formula (Any Aspect Ratio)
docs.text:
If your resolution is not listed below, calculate it with this formula.

Given:

  • BaseW = base width
  • BaseH = base height
  • FOV = selected FOV value

Output resolution:

CroppedW = (BaseW - FOV) / 2
CroppedH = (BaseH - FOV) / 2

If you need OBS Crop/Pad values (center crop):

Left   = Right  = (BaseW + FOV) / 4
Top    = Bottom = (BaseH + FOV) / 4

Validation:

  • FOV < BaseW and FOV < BaseH (otherwise output becomes zero/negative)
  • Use even numbers to avoid decimals (32-step values are commonly used)

Example (also works for 21:9):

BaseW = 5120, BaseH = 1440, FOV = 320
CroppedW = (5120 - 320) / 2 = 2400
CroppedH = (1440 - 320) / 2 = 560
Left=Right=(5120+320)/4=1360
Top=Bottom=(1440+320)/4=440

docs.title: Custom Resolution FOV Calculator
docs.text:
Enter custom width/height and select FOV to get cropped resolution and OBS Crop/Pad values.

Width (BaseW) Height (BaseH) FOV 128 160 192 224 256 288 320 352 384 416 448 480 512 544 576 608 640 Custom...

docs.title: Resolution Reference Tables
docs.text:
Select the reference table for your resolution:

  • 1920x1080 (1K)
  • 2560x1440 (2K)
  • 3440x1440 (21:9)
  • 3840x2160 (4K)

docs.title: GStreamer Installation Guide
docs.text: Shared guide index: lang.md

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: GStreamer is the runtime used when running the "Capture Card (GStreamer)" mode in CVM colorBot and is installed on the CVM machine (Sec PC). Installation is optional: if you use a capture card as your video source, installing GStreamer can improve capture quality and performance. This guide will help you install GStreamer on Windows.

docs.title: Step 1: Download GStreamer Installer
docs.text:
1. Download the GStreamer MSVC installer for Windows x64:
  2. Direct Download from Official Website: gstreamer-1.0-msvc-x86_64-1.28.0.exe

  3. Official Website: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/download/#windows

  4. The installer includes both runtime and development packages.

docs.title: Step 2: Run the Installer and Select Installation Option
docs.text:
1. Run the downloaded installer (gstreamer-1.0-msvc-x86_64-1.28.0.exe).
  2. In the installation options, select "Install for all user".
  3. Click Next to continue.

docs.title: Step 3: Welcome Page
docs.text:
1. Read the installer welcome message.
  2. Click Next to continue.

docs.title: Step 4: Accept License Agreement
docs.text:
1. Read the license agreement.
  2. Select "I accept the terms in the License Agreement".
  3. Click Next to continue.

docs.title: Step 5: Choose Installation Type
docs.text:
1. Select "Complete" installation (recommended) to install all components, including runtime and development packages.
  2. Click Next to continue.

docs.title: Step 6: Select Additional Components (Optional)
docs.text:
1. Select additional components as needed.
  2. Click Next to continue.

docs.title: Step 7: Select Installation Directory
docs.text:
1. Set the installation path to C:\Program Files\gstreamer\1.0\msvc_x86_64\.
  2. Do not change the installation directory - use the path above to ensure proper installation.
  3. Click Next to continue.

docs.title: Step 8: Ready to Install
docs.text:
1. Review the installation settings summary.
  2. Click Install to begin installation when ready.

docs.title: Step 9: Installation Complete
docs.text:
1. Wait for the installation to complete. This may take a few minutes depending on your system.
  2. Once installation is complete, click Finish to complete the installation.
  3. The installer will automatically add GStreamer to your system PATH.

docs.title: Step 10: Verify Installation
docs.text:
1. Open a new Command Prompt or PowerShell window.
  2. Run the following command to verify GStreamer is installed:
gst-launch-1.0 --version
  1. You should see output similar to:
gst-launch-1.0 version 1.28.0
GStreamer 1.28.0

docs.title: Step 11: Using GStreamer in CVM colorBot
docs.text:
1. After installation, restart CVM colorBot if it's running.
  2. In the General tab, under Capture Controls, select "Capture Card (GStreamer)" from the Method dropdown.
  3. Configure your capture card settings (Device Index, Resolution, FPS, etc.).
  4. Click CONNECT to start using GStreamer capture.

docs.title: GStreamer Not Detected
docs.text:
If CVM colorBot cannot detect GStreamer:

  1. Check PATH: Ensure GStreamer is in your system PATH. The installer should add it automatically, but you may need to restart your computer.
  2. Manual PATH: If needed, add C:\Program Files\gstreamer\1.0\msvc_x86_64\bin to your system PATH manually.
  3. Restart Application: Close and restart CVM colorBot after installation.

docs.title: Installation Issues
docs.text:
• If the installer fails, try running it as Administrator.
  • Ensure you have sufficient disk space (GStreamer requires several hundred MB).
  • Check Windows Event Viewer for detailed error messages.

docs.title: DLL Not Found Errors
docs.text:
• Ensure you downloaded the MSVC version (not MinGW) for best compatibility.
  • Verify the installation path is correct.
  • Try reinstalling if DLL errors persist.

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• GStreamer Official Documentation: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/documentation/
  • GStreamer Windows Installation Guide: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/documentation/installing/on-windows.html

docs.title: Kmbox Net Connection Guide
docs.text: Shared guide index: README.md

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Use Kmbox Net network-controlled keyboard and mouse controller in CVM. Kmbox Net is a keyboard and mouse control device that communicates over the network, featuring high speed, strong stability, and high security.

docs.title: What is Kmbox Net
docs.text:
Kmbox Net is a network-controlled keyboard and mouse controller with the following features:

  • High Security: Protocol is not public, each box has independent IP, port, and hardware encoding, uses blocking socket communication, cannot be scanned
  • Strong Stability: Will not white screen and restart like serial communication
  • High Speed: 100M dedicated network, communication speed is 100 times that of the original B board, close to 1000 calls per second
  • Automatic Default Human Trajectory: No keyboard and mouse data anomalies
  • No Mouse Adaptation Required: Strong universality
  • Supports Monitoring Physical Keyboard and Mouse: Blocks physical keyboard and mouse functions, convenient for writing software
  • Supports Physical Keyboard and Mouse Learning and Copying: Supports driver passthrough mode

Important Note: - Kmbox Net only has stronger hardware performance compared to Kmbox B - However, in terms of API, serial port API is better than Kmbox Net

Technical Information: - Kmbox Net 8K version's underlying code is based on Cherry USB

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• One Kmbox Net box
  • Two blue cables (USB cables)
  • Windows 10 or 11 system
  • Two computers (single machine mode can connect to the same computer)

docs.title: 01 Unboxing Introduction
docs.text:
Kmbox Net default includes the following accessories:

  • One Kmbox Net box
  • Two blue cables

docs.title: 02 How to Connect Cables
docs.text:
The back of the box has interface names. Generally connect according to the following diagram:

  • Game Console Interface (1): Connect to target computer (the controlled computer)
  • Network Port (2): Connect to control computer (the computer running CVM)

Note: - If it's single machine mode, both network port and game console port connect to the same computer - If computer B controls computer A, then game console interface (1) connects to computer A, network port (2) connects to computer B

After connection is complete, the Kmbox Net screen should display as shown below:

docs.title: 03 Install Network Card Driver (Install driver on computer connected to network port)
docs.text:
1. Connect the network port USB cable to the control computer (second PC)
  2. On the second PC, run 2. WCHUSBNIC driver installer as administrator
  1. Select INSTALL to install
  1. After successful installation, you should see "Driver Install Success!" message
  1. Reboot the second PC

Download Network Card Driver: Click to download network card driver

Notes: - Install the network card driver on whichever computer the network port USB cable is connected to - Don't install driver on computer A but connect cable to computer B - If network card driver is installed but no response: - Check if network port cable is connected? - Confirm driver is installed on the correct computer - Confirm running driver installer as administrator

docs.title: 04 Modify Network Card IP (Modify network card IP on computer connected to network port)
docs.text: Important: Ensure main PC and second PC are connected to the same local network (e.g., your home Wi-Fi)

docs.title: Open Control Panel → Network and Sharing Center
docs.text:
1. Open Control Panel on the second PC
  1. Click "Network and Internet"
  1. Click "Network and Sharing Center"
  1. Click "Change adapter settings"

docs.title: Modify Network Card IP
docs.text:
1. Find "USB 2.0 Ethernet Adapter" or similar network card device (this is the box's network card)

If you don't see this device, disconnect and reconnect the USB cable from Kmbox to the second PC.

  1. Right-click on this network card and select "Properties"

  2. Make sure "Internet Protocol Version 6 (TCP/IPv6)" is disabled

  1. Make sure "Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4)" is enabled, click on it and select "Properties"

  1. Select "Use the following IP address"
  2. Set IP address to: 192.168.2.100
  3. Subnet mask: 255.255.255.0
  4. Leave other fields empty
  5. Click "OK" to save

Important Notes: - Modify the box's network card, not your own local network card - Usually a new network card will appear on the page (the box's network card), modify the box's network card - If you lose internet after modifying IP, you may have modified the wrong network card - If the above IP address doesn't work, you can try using: 192.168.2.189

docs.title: Check Network Connectivity
docs.text:
1. Open Command Prompt (CMD)
  2. Execute ping 192.168.2.1 (the box's default IP)
  3. If you can ping successfully, the connection is successful

docs.title: First Connection
docs.text:
1. Open CVM's General tab
  2. Set Input API to NET or corresponding network mode
  3. When starting for the first time, you need to enter Kmbox Net data (IP, Port, UUID)

docs.title: Save Configuration
docs.text:
To avoid entering Kmbox Net data every time:

  1. Go to config section in CVM
  2. Select any config and click save

This way the settings will be saved and will be used automatically on next startup.

docs.title: Recommended Starting Values
docs.text:
• Input API: NET
  • IP Address: 192.168.2.1
  • Port: Set according to actual box port (usually default value)
  • auto_connect_mouse_api: Set to false initially, change to true after stable

docs.title: Verification Checklist
docs.text:
• CVM can connect to Kmbox Net without timeout errors
  • Micro-movement test is smooth, direction is correct
  • After restarting CVM, can connect again with the same configuration
  • Network ping test is successful

docs.title: Cannot Connect
docs.text:
• Check if network port cable is correctly connected
  • Confirm network card driver is correctly installed on the computer connected to network port
  • Confirm IP address is set correctly (192.168.2.100)
  • Confirm box's IP address (usually 192.168.2.1)
  • Check if firewall is blocking the connection
  • Confirm two computers are on the same network (except single machine mode)

docs.title: Network Card Driver Installed But No Response
docs.text:
• Check if network port cable is connected?
  • Install network card driver on whichever computer the network port USB cable is connected to
  • Don't install driver on computer A but connect cable to computer B

docs.title: Why No Internet After Modifying IP
docs.text:
• If you lost internet, you may have modified the wrong network card
  • Usually a new network card will appear on the page (the box's network card), modify the box's network card instead of your own local network card
  • See the network card modification tutorial above for specific operations

docs.title: Mouse Doesn't Work
docs.text:
If the mouse connected through Kmbox doesn't work on the main PC:

  • Make sure Kmbox is connected correctly
  • Try connecting USB cable to another port on the main PC
  • Try using another mouse
  • Try connecting mouse to another USB port on Kmbox

docs.title: Mouse Sensitivity
docs.text: Change the sensitivity in the game settings.

docs.title: Movement Not Smooth or Delayed
docs.text:
• Check network connection quality
  • Confirm using high-quality USB cables
  • Avoid using USB Hub, connect directly to computer USB port
  • Check if other programs are occupying network resources

Make sure you have configured everything correctly.

docs.title: Mouse Obviously Not Responsive and High Latency in Bypass Mode
docs.text:
• This situation is generally because the mouse exceeds specifications
  • Net only supports up to Full Speed (12Mbps). If your device is High Speed (480Mbps), Net box will only recognize it as Full Speed
  • USB protocol specifies: Full Speed devices 1ms per frame, High Speed devices 0.125ms per frame
  • If a high-speed device wants 1KHZ polling rate, its defined value is 8, but the box can only recognize Full Speed, not High Speed, so when the box reads 8, it means reading data every 8ms, thus causing high latency issues
  • For perfect passthrough support, please choose the NB version with higher speed rating
  • If you want to use high-speed devices on Net, please choose KM mode

Important Reminder: Kmbox Net's Bypass mode is suspected to be a fake Bypass, PID/VID are not correctly passed through, may not fully simulate real physical keyboard and mouse devices.

docs.title: Cheat Doesn't See Kmbox
docs.text:
If the cheat doesn't see Kmbox at startup:

  • Make sure your main and second PCs are connected to the same local network (e.g., your home Wi-Fi)
  • Try using this IP address in "Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4)" Properties: 192.168.2.189

docs.title: Firmware Update
docs.text:
If your Kmbox Net firmware differs from the current one, it's recommended to update (flash) it. This can improve performance, fix bugs, and prevent detection.

Latest Version: 2026.2.12 (Last update date: February 12, 2026)

docs.title: Update Steps
docs.text:
1. Connect USB cable to your main PC

  2. Press and hold this small black button inside the Kmbox using a screwdriver or other thin object

And at the same time connect the USB cable to this connector

The Kmbox screen should turn white, and this blue diode should start flashing rapidly. If this doesn't happen, then try to hold the button again and reconnect the USB cable.

  1. On your main PC, download and extract the upgrade tool, then install the driver

Download Upgrade Tool: Click to download upgrade tool

  1. Launch the Upgrade Tool. Click Search Device. In the information line, it should be written "Found 1 USB devices"

  1. Click Select file

  1. Navigate to the folder where you downloaded the firmware file

Download Firmware: - Latest Firmware: Click to download latest firmware - Other Versions: Click to view history versions

Change the type of files displayed to .BIN

  1. Select the firmware file (e.g., kmboxnetfw20240808.bin)

  2. And click "Program"

  1. Wait a few seconds. After the end of the process, you will see a message

Important: Firmware update is complete. Now we need to spoof Kmbox according to the guide below. This must be done every time after updating the firmware on Kmbox Net.

docs.title: Spoofing Kmbox Net
docs.text: Spoofing can make Kmbox Net appear as your real mouse, improving security.

docs.title: Spoofing Steps
docs.text:
1. Disconnect all cables and mouse from Kmbox

  2. Connect your mouse to your main PC as usual, without Kmbox

  3. On your main PC, download and run USBLogView.exe (you can download it from the official Kmbox Net resources or use a USB device viewer tool), make the program fullscreen

  1. Unplug your mouse from main PC and plug it back in

You will see Vendor ID and Product ID of your mouse

We will need this data later, write it down somewhere in one line without a space

In my case it will look like this: 046dc077

Important! On some mice, for example on my Steelseries Rival 3 Wireless, Vendor ID and Product ID contains only numbers and looks like this: 10381830. You will fail the spoofing process on such a mouse, I recommend using any other one.

  1. Connect Kmbox to your main and second PC

  1. On the second PC, download and extract the KmboxNet program

Run KmboxNet.exe

Enter IP, Port and UUID from your Kmbox Net

  1. Check "Ping" box and click on the big button

  1. A window should appear in which OK will be written at the end.

If it doesn't connect to the Kmbox: - Try to restart it by unplugging it from your main PC and plug it back in - Run the exe as admin

  1. Enter your Vendor ID and Product ID which we saved earlier in one line without a space (in my case it's 10381830)

And click VID/PID button

  1. You should see such a window which means that the process was successful

  1. Go back to your main PC and open USBLogView.exe again, make the program fullscreen

  2. Unplug the Kmbox from your main PC and plug it back in

You should see the same Vendor ID and Product ID that you saw in Step 3

If it is, then the spoofing was successful

docs.title: How to Enable Bypass Mode
docs.text:
Bypass mode allows physical keyboard and mouse to pass directly through the box. In Bypass mode, physical keyboard and mouse data is directly passed through to the target computer without processing by the box.

Important Warning: - This Bypass mode is suspected to be a fake Bypass - PID/VID are not correctly passed through, may not fully simulate real physical keyboard and mouse devices - Please be aware of related limitations and risks when using

Usage Notes: - In Bypass mode, if using high-speed devices (High Speed, 480Mbps), you may experience obvious lag and high latency issues - Net only supports up to Full Speed (12Mbps). If the device is High Speed (480Mbps), Net box will only recognize it as Full Speed - USB protocol specifies: Full Speed devices 1ms per frame, High Speed devices 0.125ms per frame - For perfect passthrough support, please choose the NB version with higher speed rating - If you want to use high-speed devices on Net, please choose KM mode

For specific setup methods, please refer to Kmbox Net Official Documentation.

docs.title: How to Enable KM Mode
docs.text: KM mode is one of Kmbox Net's working modes, which can be set in the box's host computer software.

docs.title: How to Modify Curves
docs.text:
1. Download the box's host computer control software
  2. After connecting the box, you can select hardware curve values and algorithm types
  3. Default curve is disabled. If you need to enable it, set it in the host computer software

docs.title: Check Box Inherent Parameters
docs.text: You can view the inherent parameters (IP, Port, UUID) on the box:

docs.title: Related Resources
docs.text:
• Official Documentation: Kmbox Net User Manual
  • Video Tutorial: YouTube Connection Tutorial Video
  • Network Card Driver Download: Click to download network card driver
  • Host Computer Software Download: Click to download KmboxNet host computer software

docs.title: MAKCU Setup
docs.text: Shared guide index: lang.md

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Use MAKCU hardware backend in CVM. Standard MAKCU setups mainly move through Input API = Serial; for 3.8 left (firewall) firmware or future makxdV2 hardware, MakV2Binary is recommended (MakV2 is a compatibility option).

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• MAKCU device connected and recognized by Windows.
  • Correct USB/serial driver installed.
  • CVM has permission to access serial devices.

docs.title: CVM Configuration
docs.text:
1. Open General tab.
  2. For standard MAKCU, set Input API to Serial first.
  3. If your device is 3.8 left (firewall) firmware or makxdV2, set Input API to MakV2Binary.
  4. Set Port (optional) (makv2_port) to a fixed COM port (recommended), for example COM5.
  5. Set Baud (makv2_baud) to your firmware target.
  6. Enable Auto Connect Mouse API On Startup after one successful manual connect.

docs.title: Recommended Starting Values
docs.text:
• Input API: use Serial for standard MAKCU; use MakV2Binary for 3.8 left (firewall) / makxdV2
  • makv2_port: leave empty for auto-detect first, then lock to fixed COM after validation
  • makv2_baud: 4000000
  • auto_connect_mouse_api: false during first setup, then true

docs.title: Validation Checklist
docs.text:
• CVM can connect to MAKCU without timeout.
  • Micro movement test is smooth and directional.
  • Reopen CVM and reconnect successfully with same settings.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Cannot connect:
  • Check COM port in Device Manager.
  • Close other software that may occupy the serial port.
  • If Serial fails, verify driver/port first, then switch by firmware type to MakV2Binary or MakV2.
  • Movement jitter:
  • Recheck baud rate and firmware compatibility.
  • Use high-quality USB cable and avoid USB hubs.
  • Port changes every reboot:
  • Bind device to a fixed USB port and lock makv2_port.

docs.title: Private Parameters to Fill
docs.text:
Replace with your team-specific values from Notion if required:

  • Exact MAKCU firmware version mapping
  • Required API mode (Serial / MakV2 / MakV2Binary)
  • Approved baud values per firmware line

docs.title: OBS UDP Setup
docs.text:
Shared guide index: lang.md

Follow these steps to stream your cropped FOV from the main PC to the secondary PC via UDP.

docs.title: Step 1: Configure FFmpeg URL
docs.text:
Path: Settings -> Output -> Recording

Set:

  1. Output Mode to Advanced.
  2. Type to Custom Output (FFmpeg).
  3. FFmpeg Output Type to Output to URL.
  4. File path or URL to:
udp://<sec PC IP>:<port>

Example:

udp://192.168.0.1:1234

Notes:

  • <sec PC IP> is the IPv4 address of your secondary PC.
  • <port> is any unused port you choose, and a 4-digit port is recommended (for example 1234, 2345).

docs.title: Step 2: Container and Encoding Settings
docs.text:
Use:

  1. Container Format: mjpeg (Video)
  2. Container Format Description: MIME multipart JPEG
  3. Keyframe interval (frames): 0

Other fields can stay at defaults initially.

docs.title: Step 3: Set Resolution and FPS (FOV size)
docs.text:
Path: Settings -> Video

Set:

  1. Base (Canvas) Resolution to your FOV value corresponding resolution. For example, when FOV is 128, set it to 128x128.
  2. Output (Scaled) Resolution to your FOV value corresponding resolution. For example, when FOV is 128, set it to 128x128.
  3. FPS higher than 144.

Recommended FPS values: 160 / 165 / 180 / 240 (based on GPU performance).

Important: Both Base (Canvas) Resolution and Output (Scaled) Resolution should be set to the resolution corresponding to your FOV value (e.g., when FOV = 128, set to 128x128).

Note: The table below shows the format of the FOV-size table. In the FOV-size table, Base Resolution corresponds to your desktop/game resolution, and Cropped Resolution is used to fill the OBS Filter Crop/Pad (see Step 4).

Example (FOV = 128, corresponding to FOV-size table):

FOV Base Resolution Cropped Resolution 128 1920x1080 896x476

docs.title: Step 4: Apply Crop/Pad Filter
docs.text:
To send only the FOV region:

  1. Select your Game Capture or Display Capture source in OBS.
  2. Click Filters.
  3. Add Crop/Pad.
  4. Fill crop values (Left/Top/Right/Bottom) according to your FOV-size table:
  5. The Cropped Resolution in the FOV-size table is used to fill the OBS Filter Crop/Pad values.
  6. Left and Right correspond to the first value (width) in the Cropped Resolution column of the FOV-size table.
  7. Top and Bottom correspond to the second value (height) in the Cropped Resolution column of the FOV-size table.

Example (FOV = 128, Desktop/Game Resolution = 1920x1080):

According to the FOV-size table, when desktop/game resolution is 1920x1080 and FOV is 128, Cropped Resolution is 896x476. This 896x476 is used to fill the OBS Filter Crop/Pad: - Left = 896 - Top = 476 - Right = 896 - Bottom = 476

Note: - In the FOV-size table, find the table that matches your desktop or game resolution (e.g., 1920x1080). - Then select your desired FOV value (e.g., 128). - The corresponding Cropped Resolution (e.g., 896x476) is the value used to fill the OBS Filter Crop/Pad.

This prevents oversized frames and reduces UDP drop risk.

docs.title: Step 5: Start Recording (Start UDP stream)
docs.text:
When everything is set correctly, click Start Recording.

OBS will begin sending the UDP stream from the main PC to the secondary PC.

docs.title: Secondary PC Setup
docs.text:
Use the exact same IP and port values used in OBS.

Example:

  1. OBS output URL:
udp://192.168.0.1:1234
  1. Secondary PC input:
  2. IP: 192.168.0.1
  3. Port: 1234

If any value does not match exactly, the stream will not be received.

docs.title: How to Find Secondary PC IP
docs.text:
1. Press Win + R, type cmd, and press Enter.
  2. Run:
ipconfig
  1. Find IPv4 Address under your active adapter.

docs.title: Why frame queue is empty appears
docs.text:
Common causes:

  1. Main PC and secondary PC are not connected on network.
  2. OBS recording was not started.
  3. IP or port is incorrect in UI.
  4. OBS output/filter/encoder settings are wrong.

Check connectivity both directions:

ping <secondary PC IP>
ping <main PC IP>

docs.title: Extra Note for UDP Users
docs.text: UDP still requires Crop/Pad. Without cropping, frame size can be too large for stable transport, causing empty queues or dropped frames.

docs.title: Gray screen with frequent error: Corrupt JPEG data: premature end of data segment
docs.text:
Cause: FPS is too high and decoder cannot keep up.

Fix:

  1. Lower OBS FPS.
  2. Prioritize stable decode first, then increase FPS gradually.

docs.title: FOV Size Reference
docs.text: See: FOV-size.md

docs.title: Python Installation Guide
docs.text: Shared guide index: lang.md

docs.title: Step 1: Download Python Installer
docs.text:
1. Go to the official Python download page.
  2. Download Python 3 for Windows; Python 3.11.9 is recommended.
  3. Run the installer.

Official site:

  • https://www.python.org/downloads/
  • https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3119/

docs.title: Step 2: Customize Install and Add to PATH
docs.text:
1. In the installer, click Customize installation.
  2. Make sure Python is added to PATH.
  3. Continue to the next step.

docs.title: Step 3: Advanced Options and Install
docs.text:
1. Keep pip enabled.
  2. Enable install options for all users if needed.
  3. Click Install.

docs.title: Step 4: Complete Installation
docs.text:
1. Wait for installation to finish.
  2. If shown, run path length fix.
  3. Close the installer.

docs.title: Step 5: Verify in Terminal
docs.text:
Run:

python --version
pip --version

If both commands return versions, installation is complete.

docs.text:

  • FOV Size 对照表

docs.title: CVM colorBot 安裝指南
docs.text:
共享指南索引: README.md

本指南將協助您完成 CVM colorBot 的完整安裝與設置流程。

docs.title: 前置條件
docs.text:
在開始安裝 CVM colorBot 之前，請確保您的系統滿足以下要求：

  • 作業系統: Windows 10 或更高版本
  • Python: Python 3.11 或更高版本（推薦 Python 3.11.9）
  • 網路連線: 用於下載依賴套件（首次安裝時）

如果您尚未安裝 Python，請先參考 Python 安裝指南 完成 Python 的安裝。

docs.title: 第 1 步: 下載專案
docs.text:
1. 從 GitHub 下載或克隆 CVM colorBot 專案。
  2. 將專案解壓縮到您選擇的目錄（例如 D:\CVM-colorBot-main）。
  3. 確認專案目錄中包含以下檔案：
  4. main.py（主程式入口）
  5. setup.bat（自動安裝腳本）
  6. run.bat（應用程式啟動腳本）
  7. requirements.txt（依賴套件清單）

docs.title: 第 2 步: 執行自動安裝腳本
docs.text:
1. 在專案根目錄中找到 setup.bat 檔案。

  2. 雙擊執行 setup.bat，或在命令提示字元中執行： 

    setup.bat

  3. 安裝腳本會自動執行以下操作：

  4. 檢查 Python 是否已安裝並在 PATH 中
  5. 創建虛擬環境（venv 資料夾）
  6. 升級 pip 到最新版本

  7. 安裝所有必要的依賴套件

  8. 等待安裝完成。安裝過程可能需要幾分鐘，取決於您的網路速度。

docs.title: 第 3 步: 驗證安裝
docs.text:
安裝完成後，請確認以下項目：

  1. 檢查虛擬環境：
  2. 確認專案目錄中已創建 venv 資料夾

  3. 確認 venv\Scripts\ 目錄存在

  4. 檢查依賴套件（可選）： 在命令提示字元中執行： 

    venv\Scripts\activate
pip list
    您應該能看到以下主要套件：

  5. customtkinter
  6. numpy
  7. opencv-python
  8. pyserial
  9. mss
  10. 以及其他依賴套件

docs.title: 第 4 步: 啟動應用程式
docs.text: 安裝完成後，您可以透過以下方式啟動 CVM colorBot：

docs.title: 方法 1: 使用啟動腳本（推薦）
docs.text:
1. 雙擊專案根目錄中的 run.bat 檔案。
  2. 應用程式會自動使用虛擬環境中的 Python 啟動。

docs.title: 方法 2: 手動啟動
docs.text:
1. 開啟命令提示字元（CMD）或 PowerShell。
  2. 切換到專案目錄： 
    cd D:\CVM-colorBot-main
  3. 啟動應用程式： 
    venv\Scripts\python.exe main.py

docs.title: 第 5 步: 首次配置
docs.text:
首次啟動 CVM colorBot 時，建議進行以下基本配置：

  1. 選擇擷取模式：
  2. 在 General 標籤頁中選擇適合的擷取模式
  3. 可選模式：UDP、NDI、Capture Card、MSS

  4. 詳細設置請參考對應的設置指南：

    • OBS UDP 設置
    • MAKCU 設置

  5. 配置滑鼠 API：

  6. 在 General 標籤頁中選擇 Input API
  7. 如果使用 MAKCU 設備，選擇 Serial（除非是 3.8 left (firewall) 固件或 makxdV2 硬體，則選擇 MakV2Binary）
  8. 配置相關連接參數（如 COM 埠、波特率等）

  9. 詳細設置請參考 MAKCU 設置

  10. 調整基本設定：

  11. 注意：FOV 大小是在 OBS 中調整的（參考 OBS UDP 設置 和 FOV Size 對照表），而非在 CVM 中設定
  12. 在 CVM 中配置瞄準模式和參數
  13. 根據需要啟用或停用各項功能

docs.title: 問題 1: Python 未找到
docs.text:
錯誤訊息: [ERROR] Python is not installed or not in PATH

解決方法: 1. 確認 Python 已正確安裝（執行 python --version 檢查） 2. 確認 Python 已加入系統 PATH 3. 參考 Python 安裝指南 重新安裝 Python

docs.title: 問題 2: 虛擬環境創建失敗
docs.text:
錯誤訊息: [ERROR] Failed to create virtual environment

解決方法: 1. 確認有足夠的磁碟空間 2. 確認專案目錄有寫入權限 3. 嘗試以系統管理員身分執行 setup.bat 4. 檢查 Python 安裝是否完整

docs.title: 問題 3: 依賴套件安裝失敗
docs.text:
錯誤訊息: [ERROR] Failed to install dependencies

解決方法: 1. 檢查網路連線是否正常 2. 確認 pip 已更新到最新版本： 

python -m pip install --upgrade pip
3. 嘗試手動安裝依賴套件： 
venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
4. 如果特定套件安裝失敗，可以嘗試單獨安裝： 
pip install <套件名稱>

docs.title: 問題 4: 應用程式無法啟動
docs.text:
錯誤訊息: [ERROR] Program exited with error

解決方法: 1. 確認虛擬環境已正確創建 2. 確認所有依賴套件已正確安裝 3. 檢查 config.json 檔案是否存在且格式正確 4. 查看控制台輸出的詳細錯誤訊息 5. 確認系統滿足所有前置條件

docs.title: 問題 5: 模組導入錯誤
docs.text:
錯誤訊息: ModuleNotFoundError 或 ImportError

解決方法: 1. 確認您使用的是虛擬環境中的 Python： 

venv\Scripts\python.exe main.py
2. 重新安裝缺失的套件： 
venv\Scripts\activate
pip install <缺失的套件名稱>
3. 如果問題持續，嘗試重新執行 setup.bat

docs.title: 後續步驟
docs.text:
安裝完成後，建議您：

  1. 閱讀相關設置指南：
  2. OBS UDP 設置 - 配置 UDP 擷取模式
  3. MAKCU 設置 - 配置 MAKCU 硬體設備
  4. Kmbox Net 連接教學 - 配置 Kmbox Net 設備

  5. FOV Size 對照表 - 了解 FOV 尺寸設定

  6. 熟悉應用程式介面：

  7. 探索各個標籤頁的功能
  8. 了解各項配置選項的用途

  9. 測試基本功能是否正常運作

  10. 優化設定：

  11. 根據您的硬體和需求調整參數
  12. 保存配置檔案以便日後使用
  13. 測試不同模式以找到最適合的設定

docs.title: 注意事項
docs.text:
• 僅支援 Windows 系統: CVM colorBot 專為 Windows 設計，不支援其他作業系統
  • 定期更新: 建議定期檢查並更新專案和依賴套件
  • 備份配置: 定期備份您的 config.json 和配置檔案
  • 系統權限: 某些功能可能需要系統管理員權限（如串口存取）

docs.title: 需要協助？
docs.text:
如果您在安裝過程中遇到問題：

  1. 檢查本文檔的「故障排查」章節
  2. 查看專案的 GitHub Issues
  3. 確認您的系統和 Python 版本符合要求
  4. 提供詳細的錯誤訊息以便診斷問題

docs.title: FOV Size 对照表
docs.text:
共享指南索引: README.md

以下表格用于根据基础分辨率选择对应裁剪输出分辨率。

docs.title: 如何使用
docs.text:
Base Resolution 對應你的桌面或遊戲解析度。根據你的桌面解析度找到對應的表格，然後選擇你要的 FOV 值。

示例: - 如果你的桌面解析度是 2560x1440，請查看「## 2560x1440」表格 - 然後選擇你要的 FOV 值，例如 128 - 對應的 Cropped Resolution 為 1216x656，這就是你在 OBS Crop/Pad 濾鏡中需要填寫的裁剪值

docs.title: 通用算法（适用于任意画面比例）
docs.text:
如果你的分辨率不在下方表格中，可以按下面算法自己计算。

已知:

  • BaseW = 基础宽度（Base Resolution Width）
  • BaseH = 基础高度（Base Resolution Height）
  • FOV = 你要使用的 FOV 值

计算输出分辨率:

CroppedW = (BaseW - FOV) / 2
CroppedH = (BaseH - FOV) / 2

如果你要填写 OBS Crop/Pad（居中裁剪）:

Left   = Right  = (BaseW + FOV) / 4
Top    = Bottom = (BaseH + FOV) / 4

校验条件:

  • FOV < BaseW 且 FOV < BaseH（否则计算结果会变成 0 或负数）
  • 建议使用偶数值，避免出现小数（常见使用 32 的倍数）

示例（21:9 也适用）:

BaseW = 5120, BaseH = 1440, FOV = 320
CroppedW = (5120 - 320) / 2 = 2400
CroppedH = (1440 - 320) / 2 = 560
Left=Right=(5120+320)/4=1360
Top=Bottom=(1440+320)/4=440

docs.title: 自訂解析度 FOV 計算器
docs.text:
輸入自訂寬高、選擇 FOV，即時計算裁剪解析度與 OBS Crop/Pad 數值。

寬度 (BaseW) 高度 (BaseH) FOV 128 160 192 224 256 288 320 352 384 416 448 480 512 544 576 608 640 自訂...

docs.title: GStreamer 安裝指南
docs.text: 共享指南索引: lang.md

docs.title: 概述
docs.text: GStreamer 是在 CVM colorBot 中使用「Capture Card (GStreamer)」模式時的運行時，安裝在 CVM 機（Sec PC）。此為可選安裝：若您使用擷取卡作為影像來源，安裝 GStreamer 可獲得更好的擷取效果。本指南將幫助您在 Windows 上安裝 GStreamer。

docs.title: 第 1 步: 下載 GStreamer 安裝程序
docs.text:
1. 下載適用於 Windows x64 的 GStreamer MSVC 安裝程序：
  2. 從官方網站直接下載: gstreamer-1.0-msvc-x86_64-1.28.0.exe

  3. 官方網站: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/download/#windows

  4. 安裝程序包含運行時和開發包。

docs.title: 第 2 步: 運行安裝程序並選擇安裝選項
docs.text:
1. 運行下載的安裝程序 (gstreamer-1.0-msvc-x86_64-1.28.0.exe)。
  2. 在安裝選項中，選擇 「Install for all user」（為所有用戶安裝）。
  3. 點擊 Next 繼續。

docs.title: 第 3 步: 歡迎頁面
docs.text:
1. 閱讀安裝程序歡迎訊息。
  2. 點擊 Next 繼續。

docs.title: 第 4 步: 接受許可協議
docs.text:
1. 閱讀許可協議。
  2. 選擇 「I accept the terms in the License Agreement」（我接受許可協議中的條款）。
  3. 點擊 Next 繼續。

docs.title: 第 5 步: 選擇安裝類型
docs.text:
1. 選擇 「Complete」（完整） 安裝（推薦）以安裝所有組件，包括運行時和開發包。
  2. 點擊 Next 繼續。

docs.title: 第 6 步: 選擇附加組件（可選）
docs.text:
1. 根據需要選擇附加組件。
  2. 點擊 Next 繼續。

docs.title: 第 7 步: 選擇安裝目錄
docs.text:
1. 請將安裝路徑設置為 C:\Program Files\gstreamer\1.0\msvc_x86_64\。
  2. 請不要更改安裝目錄，使用上述路徑以確保正確安裝。
  3. 點擊 Next 繼續。

docs.title: 第 8 步: 準備安裝
docs.text:
1. 檢查安裝設置摘要。
  2. 確認無誤後，點擊 Install 開始安裝。

docs.title: 第 9 步: 安裝完成
docs.text:
1. 等待安裝完成。根據系統性能，可能需要幾分鐘。
  2. 安裝完成後，點擊 Finish 完成安裝。
  3. 安裝程序會自動將 GStreamer 添加到系統 PATH。

docs.title: 第 10 步: 驗證安裝
docs.text:
1. 打開新的命令提示符或 PowerShell 窗口。
  2. 運行以下命令以驗證 GStreamer 是否已安裝：
gst-launch-1.0 --version
  1. 您應該看到類似以下的輸出：
gst-launch-1.0 version 1.28.0
GStreamer 1.28.0

docs.title: 第 11 步: 在 CVM colorBot 中使用 GStreamer
docs.text:
1. 安裝完成後，如果 CVM colorBot 正在運行，請重新啟動它。
  2. 在 General 標籤頁中，在 Capture Controls 下，從 Method 下拉選單中選擇 「Capture Card (GStreamer)」。
  3. 配置您的擷取卡設置（Device Index、Resolution、FPS 等）。
  4. 點擊 CONNECT 開始使用 GStreamer 擷取。

docs.title: GStreamer 無法檢測
docs.text:
如果 CVM colorBot 無法檢測到 GStreamer：

  1. 檢查 PATH: 確保 GStreamer 在您的系統 PATH 中。安裝程序應該會自動添加，但您可能需要重新啟動計算機。
  2. 手動添加 PATH: 如有需要，手動將 C:\Program Files\gstreamer\1.0\msvc_x86_64\bin 添加到系統 PATH。
  3. 重啟應用程序: 安裝後關閉並重新啟動 CVM colorBot。

docs.title: 安裝問題
docs.text:
• 如果安裝程序失敗，請嘗試以管理員身份運行。
  • 確保您有足夠的磁盤空間（GStreamer 需要幾百 MB）。
  • 檢查 Windows 事件查看器以獲取詳細的錯誤訊息。

docs.title: DLL 未找到錯誤
docs.text:
• 確保您下載的是 MSVC 版本（不是 MinGW）以獲得最佳兼容性。
  • 驗證安裝路徑是否正確。
  • 如果 DLL 錯誤持續存在，請嘗試重新安裝。

docs.title: 其他資源
docs.text:
• GStreamer 官方文檔: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/documentation/
  • GStreamer Windows 安裝指南: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/documentation/installing/on-windows.html

docs.title: Kmbox Net 連接教學
docs.text: 共享指南索引: README.md

docs.title: 目標
docs.text: 在 CVM 中使用 Kmbox Net 網絡程控鍵鼠控制器。Kmbox Net 是一款通過網絡通信的鍵鼠控制設備，具有速度快、穩定性強、安全性高等特點。

docs.title: 什麼是 Kmbox Net
docs.text:
Kmbox Net 是一款網絡程控鍵鼠控制器，具有以下特性：

  • 安全性高：協議不公開，每個盒子獨立 IP、端口、硬件編碼，採用阻塞 socket 通信，無法被掃描
  • 穩定性強：不會像串口通信那樣白屏重啟
  • 速度快：100M 專用網絡，通信速度是原來 B 板的 100 倍，每秒調用次數接近 1000 次
  • 自動默認人工軌跡：不會出現鍵鼠數據異常
  • 無需適配鼠標：通用性強
  • 支持監控物理鍵鼠：屏蔽物理鍵鼠功能，方便寫軟件
  • 支持物理鍵鼠學習複製功能：支持驅動直通模式

重要說明： - Kmbox Net 只是相對 Kmbox B 的硬體性能較強 - 但是以 API 來說，串口的 API 比 Kmbox Net 好

技術資訊： - Kmbox Net 的 8K 版本底層代碼基於 Cherry USB

docs.title: 前置條件
docs.text:
• Kmbox Net 盒子一個
  • 藍色線纜兩條（USB 線）
  • Windows 10 或 11 系統
  • 兩台電腦（單機模式可接同一台電腦）

docs.title: 01 開箱介紹
docs.text:
Kmbox Net 默認包含以下配件：

  • Kmbox Net 盒子一個
  • 藍色線纜兩條

docs.title: 02 如何接線
docs.text:
盒子背面有接口名稱，一般按照下圖接線：

  • 遊戲機接口（1）：接目標電腦（被控制的電腦）
  • 網口（2）：接控制電腦（運行 CVM 的電腦）

注意： - 如果是單機模式，那麼網口和遊戲機口都接同一台電腦 - 如果是 B 電腦控 A 電腦，那麼遊戲機接口（1）接 A 電腦，網口（2）接 B 電腦

連接完成後，Kmbox Net 屏幕應該顯示如下圖：

docs.title: 03 安裝網卡驅動（接網口的電腦裝驅動）
docs.text:
1. 將網口 USB 線連接到控制電腦（第二台電腦）
  2. 在第二台電腦上，以管理員身份運行 2. WCHUSBNIC 驅動安裝程序
  1. 選擇 INSTALL 進行安裝
  1. 安裝成功後，您應該看到 "Driver Install Success!" 提示
  1. 重啟第二台電腦

下載網卡驅動：點我下載網卡驅動

注意事項： - 網口 USB 線接在哪裡，哪裡就裝網卡驅動 - 不要驅動裝了電腦 A，線接在電腦 B 上 - 若裝上網卡驅動沒反應： - 查看網口線接上沒有？ - 確認驅動安裝在正確的電腦上 - 確認以管理員身份運行驅動安裝程序

docs.title: 04 修改網卡 IP（接網口的電腦改網卡 IP）
docs.text: 重要：確保主電腦和第二台電腦連接到同一個本地網絡（例如，您的家庭 Wi-Fi）

docs.title: 打開控制面板 → 網絡共享中心
docs.text:
1. 在第二台電腦上打開控制面板
  1. 點擊「網絡和 Internet」
  1. 點擊「網絡和共享中心」
  1. 點擊「更改適配器設置」

docs.title: 修改網卡 IP
docs.text:
1. 找到「USB 2.0 以太網適配器」或類似的網卡設備（這是盒子的網卡）

如果看不到此設備，請斷開並重新連接 Kmbox 到第二台電腦的 USB 線。

  1. 右鍵點擊該網卡，選擇「屬性」

  2. 確保「Internet 協議版本 6 (TCP/IPv6)」已禁用

  1. 確保「Internet 協議版本 4 (TCP/IPv4)」已啟用，點擊它並選擇「屬性」

  1. 選擇「使用下面的 IP 地址」
  2. 設置 IP 地址為：192.168.2.100
  3. 子網掩碼：255.255.255.0
  4. 其他欄位留空
  5. 點擊「確定」保存

重要提示： - 要改盒子的網卡，而不是你自己的本機網卡 - 通常頁面上會新出現網卡（盒子的網卡），要改盒子的網卡 - 如果改完 IP 突然沒網了，可能是改錯了網卡 - 如果上述 IP 地址不工作，可以嘗試使用：192.168.2.189

docs.title: 檢查網絡是否連通
docs.text:
1. 打開命令提示符（CMD）
  2. 執行 ping 192.168.2.1（盒子的默認 IP）
  3. 如果能夠 ping 通，說明連接成功

docs.title: 首次連接
docs.text:
1. 打開 CVM 的 General 標籤頁
  2. 將 Input API 設置為 NET 或對應的網絡模式
  3. 首次啟動時，您需要輸入 Kmbox Net 的數據（IP、端口、UUID）

docs.title: 保存配置
docs.text:
為了避免每次都要輸入 Kmbox Net 數據，請：

  1. 在 CVM 中進入配置（config）部分
  2. 選擇任意配置並點擊保存

這樣設置就會被保存，下次啟動時會自動使用。

docs.title: 建議起始值
docs.text:
• Input API: NET
  • IP 地址: 192.168.2.1
  • 端口: 根據盒子實際端口設置（通常為默認值）
  • auto_connect_mouse_api: 首次設置為 false，穩定後改為 true

docs.title: 驗證清單
docs.text:
• CVM 可以連接到 Kmbox Net 且無超時報錯
  • 微動測試平滑，方向正確
  • 重啟 CVM 後可用同一配置再次連接
  • 網絡 ping 測試成功

docs.title: 無法連接
docs.text:
• 檢查網口線是否正確連接
  • 確認網卡驅動已正確安裝在接網口的電腦上
  • 確認 IP 地址設置正確（192.168.2.100）
  • 確認盒子的 IP 地址（通常為 192.168.2.1）
  • 檢查防火牆是否阻止了連接
  • 確認兩台電腦在同一網絡（單機模式除外）

docs.title: 裝上網卡驅動還是沒反應
docs.text:
• 查看網口線接上沒有？
  • 網口 USB 線接在哪裡，哪裡就裝網卡驅動
  • 不要驅動裝了電腦 A，線接在電腦 B 上

docs.title: 改完 IP 為什麼突然沒網了
docs.text:
• 如果沒網了，可能是改錯了網卡
  • 通常頁面上會新出現網卡（盒子的網卡），要改盒子的網卡而不是你自己的本機網卡
  • 具體操作參見上面的修改網卡教程

docs.title: 鼠標不工作
docs.text:
如果通過 Kmbox 連接的鼠標在主電腦上不工作：

  • 確保 Kmbox 連接正確
  • 嘗試將 USB 線連接到主電腦的另一個端口
  • 嘗試使用另一個鼠標
  • 嘗試將鼠標連接到 Kmbox 的另一個 USB 端口

docs.title: 鼠標靈敏度
docs.text: 在遊戲中更改靈敏度設置。

docs.title: 移動不流暢或延遲
docs.text:
• 檢查網絡連接質量
  • 確認使用高質量 USB 線纜
  • 避免使用 USB Hub，直接連接電腦 USB 口
  • 檢查是否有其他程序佔用網絡資源

確保您已正確配置所有內容

docs.title: 在 bypass 模式下鼠標明顯不跟手，延遲大
docs.text:
• 這種情況一般是因為鼠標超綱了
  • Net 僅支持到 Full Speed (12Mbps)，如果你的設備是 High Speed (480Mbps)，Net 盒子只會認為他是 Full Speed
  • USB 協議規定：Full Speed 設備 1ms 一幀，High Speed 設備 0.125ms 一幀
  • 如果高速設備要 1KHZ 回報率，其定義值是 8，但盒子只能識別到 Full Speed，不能識別 High Speed，故盒子讀取到 8 意味著 8ms 讀一次數據，因此出現延遲大的問題
  • 如需完美支持透傳，請選擇速度等級更高的 NB 版本
  • 如想要在 Net 上使用高速設備，請選擇 KM 模式

重要提醒：Kmbox Net 的 Bypass 模式疑似是虛假的 Bypass，PID/VID 並沒有正確穿透，可能無法完全模擬真實的物理鍵鼠設備。

docs.title: 固件更新
docs.text:
如果您的 Kmbox Net 固件版本與當前版本不同，建議更新（刷寫）固件。這可以提高性能、修復錯誤並防止檢測。

最新版本：2026.2.12（更新日期：2026年2月12日）

docs.title: 更新步驟
docs.text:
1. 將 USB 線連接到主電腦

  2. 使用螺絲刀或其他細小物體按住 Kmbox 內部的小黑按鈕

同時將 USB 線連接到此接口

Kmbox 屏幕應該變白，藍色指示燈應該快速閃爍。如果沒有發生，請重試按住按鈕並重新連接 USB 線。

  1. 在主電腦上，下載並解壓升級工具，然後安裝驅動

下載升級工具：點我下載升級工具

  1. 啟動升級工具，點擊「Search Device」。在信息行中應該顯示 "Found 1 USB devices"

  1. 點擊「Select file」

  1. 導航到您下載固件文件的文件夾

下載固件： - 最新固件：點我下載最新固件 - 其他版本固件：點我查看歷史版本

將文件顯示類型更改為 .BIN

  1. 選擇固件文件（例如：kmboxnetfw20240808.bin）

  2. 點擊「Program」

  1. 等待幾秒鐘。過程結束後，您會看到成功消息

重要：固件更新完成後，必須按照下面的 Spoofing 指南進行偽裝。每次更新 Kmbox Net 固件後都必須執行此操作。

docs.title: Spoofing（偽裝）Kmbox Net
docs.text: Spoofing 可以讓 Kmbox Net 偽裝成您的真實鼠標，提高安全性。

docs.title: Spoofing 步驟
docs.text:
1. 斷開 Kmbox 的所有線纜和鼠標

  2. 將鼠標直接連接到主電腦（不使用 Kmbox）

  3. 在主電腦上，下載並運行 USBLogView.exe（您可以從 Kmbox Net 官方資源下載或使用 USB 設備查看工具），將程序全屏顯示

  1. 從主電腦拔下鼠標並重新插入

您將看到鼠標的 Vendor ID 和 Product ID

將此數據寫下來（一行，無空格）

例如：046dc077

重要：某些鼠標（例如 Steelseries Rival 3 Wireless）的 Vendor ID 和 Product ID 只包含數字，例如：10381830。在這樣的鼠標上進行 Spoofing 會失敗，建議使用其他鼠標。

  1. 將 Kmbox 連接到主電腦和第二台電腦

  1. 在第二台電腦上，下載並解壓 KmboxNet 程序

運行 KmboxNet.exe

輸入 Kmbox Net 的 IP、Port 和 UUID

  1. 勾選「Ping」框並點擊大按鈕

  1. 應該會出現一個窗口，最後寫著 OK

如果無法連接到 Kmbox： - 嘗試通過從主電腦拔下並重新插入來重啟它 - 以管理員身份運行 exe

  1. 輸入您之前保存的 Vendor ID 和 Product ID（一行，無空格，例如：10381830）

點擊「VID/PID」按鈕

  1. 您應該看到一個窗口，表示過程成功

  1. 返回主電腦，再次打開 USBLogView.exe，將程序全屏顯示

  2. 從主電腦拔下 Kmbox 並重新插入

您應該看到與步驟 3 中相同的 Vendor ID 和 Product ID

如果是這樣，則 Spoofing 成功

docs.title: 如何開啟 Bypass 模式
docs.text:
Bypass 模式允許物理鍵鼠直接通過盒子傳輸。在 Bypass 模式下，物理鍵鼠的數據會直接透傳到目標電腦，而不經過盒子的處理。

重要警告： - 此 Bypass 模式疑似是虛假的 Bypass - PID/VID 並沒有正確穿透，可能無法完全模擬真實的物理鍵鼠設備 - 在使用時請注意相關限制和風險

使用注意事項： - 在 Bypass 模式下，如果使用高速設備（High Speed, 480Mbps），可能會出現明顯不跟手、延遲大的問題 - Net 僅支持到 Full Speed (12Mbps)，如果設備是 High Speed (480Mbps)，Net 盒子只會認為它是 Full Speed - USB 協議規定：Full Speed 設備 1ms 一幀，High Speed 設備 0.125ms 一幀 - 如需完美支持透傳，請選擇速度等級更高的 NB 版本 - 如想要在 Net 上使用高速設備，請選擇 KM 模式

具體設置方法請參考 Kmbox Net 官方文檔。

docs.title: 如何開啟 KM 模式
docs.text: KM 模式是 Kmbox Net 的工作模式之一，可在盒子上位機軟件中設置。

docs.title: 如何修改曲線
docs.text:
1. 下載盒子上位機控制軟件
  2. 連接盒子後，可以選擇硬件曲線值和算法類型
  3. 默認曲線是關閉的，如需開啟請在上位機軟件中設置

docs.title: 檢查盒子固有參數
docs.text: 您可以在盒子上查看固有參數（IP、Port、UUID）：

docs.title: 相關資源
docs.text:
• 官方文檔：Kmbox Net 用戶手冊
  • 視頻教學：YouTube 連接教學視頻
  • 網卡驅動下載：點我下載網卡驅動
  • 上位機軟件下載：點我下載 KmboxNet 上位機軟件

docs.title: MAKCU 设置
docs.text: 共享指南索引: README.md

docs.title: 目标
docs.text: 在 CVM 中使用 MAKCU 后端。常规 MAKCU 主要通过 Input API = Serial 移动；若为 3.8 left (firewall) 固件或未来 makxdV2 硬件，推荐使用 MakV2Binary（MakV2 作为兼容选项）。

docs.title: 前置条件
docs.text:
• MAKCU 设备已连接并被 Windows 正确识别。
  • 已安装对应 USB/串口驱动。
  • CVM 具备访问串口设备权限。

docs.title: CVM 配置步骤
docs.text:
1. 打开 General 标签页。
  2. 常规 MAKCU 先将 Input API 设为 Serial。
  3. 若设备是 3.8 left (firewall) 固件或 makxdV2，将 Input API 设为 MakV2Binary。
  4. Port (optional)（makv2_port）建议先自动识别，稳定后固定为具体 COM（如 COM5）。
  5. Baud（makv2_baud）设置为固件要求值。
  6. 首次手动连接成功后，再开启 Auto Connect Mouse API On Startup。

docs.title: 建议起始值
docs.text:
• Input API: 常规 MAKCU 用 Serial；3.8 left (firewall) / makxdV2 用 MakV2Binary
  • makv2_port: 首次可留空，验证后固定 COM
  • makv2_baud: 4000000
  • auto_connect_mouse_api: 首次 false，稳定后改 true

docs.title: 验证清单
docs.text:
• CVM 可连接 MAKCU 且无超时报错。
  • 微动测试平滑，方向正确。
  • 重启 CVM 后可用同一配置再次连接。

docs.title: 故障排查
docs.text:
• 无法连接:
  • 在设备管理器确认 COM 端口。
  • 关闭占用串口的其他软件。
  • Serial 不通时先核对驱动与端口，再按固件类型改试 MakV2Binary 或 MakV2。
  • 移动抖动:
  • 核对波特率与固件是否匹配。
  • 更换高质量 USB 线，避免 USB Hub。
  • 每次重启端口变化:
  • 将设备固定在同一 USB 口，并锁定 makv2_port。

docs.title: 需要你补充的私有参数
docs.text:
请按团队 Notion 补充：

  • MAKCU 固件版本对应关系
  • 固件要求的 API 模式（Serial / MakV2 / MakV2Binary）
  • 每个固件线允许的波特率

docs.title: OBS UDP 设置
docs.text:
共享指南索引: README.md

按照以下步骤将裁剪后的 FOV 通过 UDP 从主机发送到副机。

docs.title: 第 1 步: 配置 FFmpeg URL
docs.text:
路径: Settings -> Output -> Recording

配置如下:

  1. Output Mode 设为 Advanced。
  2. Type 设为 Custom Output (FFmpeg)。
  3. FFmpeg Output Type 设为 Output to URL。
  4. File path or URL 填写:
udp://<sec PC IP>:<port>

示例:

udp://192.168.0.1:1234

说明:

  • <sec PC IP> 是副机 IPv4 地址。
  • <port> 是你选择的未占用端口，推荐使用四位数端口（如 1234、2345）。

docs.title: 第 2 步: 容器与编码设置
docs.text:
建议使用:

  1. Container Format: mjpeg (Video)
  2. Container Format Description: MIME multipart JPEG
  3. Keyframe interval (frames): 0

其它字段可先保持默认。

docs.title: 第 3 步: 设置分辨率与 FPS（FOV 尺寸）
docs.text:
路径: Settings -> Video

配置如下:

  1. Base (Canvas) Resolution 设置为你的 FOV 值对应的分辨率。例如，当 FOV 为 128 时，设置为 128x128。
  2. Output (Scaled) Resolution 设置为你的 FOV 值对应的分辨率。例如，当 FOV 为 128 时，设置为 128x128。
  3. FPS 设置为高于 144，建议 160 / 165 / 180 / 240（按 GPU 性能选择）。

重要: Base (Canvas) Resolution 和 Output (Scaled) Resolution 都要設置成 FOV 值對應的分辨率（例如 FOV = 128 時，設置為 128x128）。

說明: 以下表格展示的是 FOV-size 表的格式。在 FOV-size 表中，Base Resolution 對應你的桌面/遊戲解析度，Cropped Resolution 用於填寫 OBS Filter 的 Crop/Pad（見第 4 步）。

示例（FOV = 128，對應 FOV-size 表）:

FOV Base Resolution Cropped Resolution 128 1920x1080 896x476

docs.title: 第 4 步: 添加 Crop/Pad 滤镜
docs.text:
为确保只传输 FOV 区域，按以下操作:

  1. 在 OBS 里选中 Game Capture 或 Display Capture 源。
  2. 点击 Filters。
  3. 添加 Crop/Pad。
  4. 按你们的 FOV-size 表填写裁剪值（Left/Top/Right/Bottom）:
  5. FOV-size 表中的 Cropped Resolution 是用來填寫 OBS Filter 的 Crop/Pad 的。
  6. Left 和 Right 对应 FOV-size 表中 Cropped Resolution 的第一个值（宽度）。
  7. Top 和 Bottom 对应 FOV-size 表中 Cropped Resolution 的第二个值（高度）。

示例（FOV = 128，桌面/遊戲解析度 = 1920x1080）:

根据 FOV-size 表，當桌面/遊戲解析度為 1920x1080、FOV 為 128 時，Cropped Resolution 为 896x476。這個 896x476 是用來填寫 OBS Filter 的 Crop/Pad: - Left = 896 - Top = 476 - Right = 896 - Bottom = 476

注意: - 在 FOV-size 表中，根據你的桌面或遊戲解析度（例如 1920x1080）找到對應的表格。 - 然後選擇你要的 FOV 值（例如 128）。 - 對應的 Cropped Resolution（例如 896x476）就是用來填寫 OBS Filter 的 Crop/Pad 的值。

这样可以避免发送过大帧，降低 UDP 丢帧风险。

docs.title: 第 5 步: 开始录制（即开始 UDP 发送）
docs.text:
参数确认后，点击 Start Recording。

OBS 会从主机向副机发送 UDP 视频流。

docs.title: 副机（Secondary PC）设置
docs.text:
副机里填写的 IP 与端口，必须和 OBS URL 完全一致。

示例:

  1. OBS URL:
udp://192.168.0.1:1234
  1. 副机配置:
  2. IP: 192.168.0.1
  3. Port: 1234

只要任一项不一致，副机就无法收到流。

docs.title: 如何查看副机 IP 地址
docs.text:
1. 按 Win + R，输入 cmd，回车。
  2. 输入以下命令并回车:
ipconfig
  1. 在网卡信息中找到 IPv4 Address，该值就是副机 IP。

docs.title: 为什么会出现 frame queue is empty
docs.text:
常见原因如下:

  1. 主机与副机网络不通。
  2. OBS 没有点击 Start Recording。
  3. UI 中 IP 或端口填写错误。
  4. OBS 输出、滤镜或编码配置错误。

网络联通性建议双向检查:

ping <secondary PC IP>
ping <main PC IP>

docs.title: UDP 用户额外注意
docs.text: UDP 模式下仍然必须做 Crop/Pad。不裁剪会导致帧尺寸过大，网络难以稳定传输，容易出现空队列或丢帧。

docs.title: 灰屏并频繁报错: Corrupt JPEG data: premature end of data segment
docs.text:
原因: FPS 设置过高，系统解码跟不上。

解决:

  1. 降低 OBS FPS。
  2. 优先保证稳定，再逐步上调到可承受值。

docs.title: FOV Size 对照表
docs.text: 请查看: FOV-size.md

docs.title: Python 安装指南
docs.text: 共享指南索引: lang.md

docs.title: 第 1 步: 下载 Python 安装包
docs.text:
1. 打开 Python 官网下载页。
  2. 下载 Python 3（Windows），推荐 Python 3.11.9。
  3. 运行安装程序。

官网:

  • https://www.python.org/downloads/
  • https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3119/

docs.title: 第 2 步: 自定义安装并加入 PATH
docs.text:
1. 在安装界面点击 Customize installation。
  2. 确认已勾选加入 PATH。
  3. 进入下一步。

docs.title: 第 3 步: 高级选项并安装
docs.text:
1. 保持 pip 启用。
  2. 需要时可启用对所有用户安装。
  3. 点击 Install 开始安装。

docs.title: 第 4 步: 完成安装
docs.text:
1. 等待安装完成。
  2. 如果出现路径长度修复提示，执行修复。
  3. 关闭安装器。

docs.title: 第 5 步: 终端验证
docs.text:
执行:

python --version
pip --version

如果两个命令都返回版本号，表示安装完成。
